rationale: I divided Biology into 14 practitioner-facing subspecialties to balance breadth and precision: common lab-focused careers (Cell Biology, Molecular Biology, Genetics, Developmental Biology) are separate from organismal/practical areas (Microbiology, Immunology, Virology, Plant Biology) and from method- or technique-centered communities (Genomics & Computational Biology, Proteomics & Metabolomics, Structural Biology, Biophysics). I kept systems- and engineering-oriented topics (Systems Biology, Synthetic Biology) distinct because they attract different readers and paper types. I deliberately did not collapse Genomics & Computational Biology into Genetics (many high-throughput/analysis papers are read by a different audience) and kept Immunology separate from Microbiology and Virology despite overlap; I avoided evolution/ecology and clinical/neurology splits because those map to other level-1 categories (Ecology & Evolution, Medicine, Brain & Cognitive Sciences).

subspecialties.name: Cell Biology
subspecialties.tagline: Mechanisms of eukaryotic cell structure, trafficking and behavior
covers: cytoskeleton, membrane trafficking, organelle biology
live-cell imaging, cell signaling, cell polarity examples: stem cell niche imaging, endocytosis mechanism papers
explicitly_excludes: Neuroscience (Brain & Cognitive Sciences) — neuronal systems work
Clinical translation (Medicine) — clinical cell therapy trials

subspecialties.name: Molecular Biology
subspecialties.tagline: Molecular mechanisms: DNA/RNA/protein interactions and regulation
covers: transcription, RNA processing, translation, post‑translational regulation
biochemical mechanism studies, molecular assays — e.g., transcription factor function, RNA-binding protein work
explicitly_excludes: Genomics & Computational Biology — large-scale sequencing/analysis papers
Structural Biology — atomic structures and cryo-EM-focused papers

subspecialties.name: Genetics
subspecialties.tagline: Inheritance, gene function, mapping and genetic screens
covers: classical and molecular genetics, mutational analysis, forward/reverse genetic screens
model-organism genetics examples: Drosophila screens, yeast gene function studies
explicitly_excludes: Genomics & Computational Biology — population-scale sequencing and computational analyses
Clinical genetics (Medicine) — clinical diagnostic/genetic counseling studies

subspecialties.name: Developmental Biology
subspecialties.tagline: Mechanisms of embryogenesis, morphogenesis and cell fate
covers: pattern formation, axis specification, organogenesis
model-organism developmental examples: zebrafish/fruit fly embryogenesis, stem cell differentiation

subspecialties.name: Microbiology
subspecialties.tagline: Bacterial and archaeal biology, physiology and ecology at the micro scale
covers: bacterial cell biology, motility, biofilms, microbial physiology
examples: bacterial pathogenesis mechanisms, microbiome mechanistic studies (non-ecosystem)

subspecialties.name: Immunology
subspecialties.tagline: Innate and adaptive immune system mechanisms and regulation
covers: immune cell signaling, antigen presentation, immune development
examples: T cell biology, vaccine mechanism studies (basic, non-clinical)
explicitly_excludes: Virology — virus-focused pathogenesis (unless immune mechanism primary)

subspecialties.name: Virology
subspecialties.tagline: Virus biology, host interactions and replication strategies
covers: viral entry/replication, host–virus molecular interactions
examples: coronavirus replication mechanism, viral vector biology (basic research)

subspecialties.name: Structural Biology
subspecialties.tagline: Atomic and near-atomic structures of biomolecules and complexes
covers: X-ray crystallography, cryo-EM, NMR structural determination
examples: protein complexes, ribosome structures, structural enzymology
explicitly_excludes: Biophysics — primarily physical modeling/mechanistic force studies

subspecialties.name: Biophysics
subspecialties.tagline: Physical principles and quantitative experiments in living systems
covers: single-molecule methods, force measurements, quantitative modeling of biological systems
examples: membrane mechanics, single-molecule motor studies
explicitly_excludes: Pure computational/machine-learning methods (Computer Science/Genomics & Computational Biology)

subspecialties.name: Systems Biology
subspecialties.tagline: Network-level, quantitative and integrative studies of biological systems
covers: network modeling, multi-omic integration, dynamic systems analysis
examples: signaling network inference, quantitative cell-population models

subspecialties.name: Synthetic Biology
subspecialties.tagline: Design, engineering and construction of biological systems and parts
covers: genetic circuits, engineered organisms, biological design principles
examples: CRISPR-based gene circuits, engineered metabolic pathways

subspecialties.name: Plant Biology
subspecialties.tagline: Molecular and cellular biology of plants and algae
covers: plant development, photosynthesis, plant molecular genetics
examples: Arabidopsis developmental studies, algal photosystems

subspecialties.name: Genomics & Computational Biology
subspecialties.tagline: High-throughput sequencing, omics analysis and computational methods
covers: genome sequencing, comparative genomics, algorithmic/ML analysis of biological data
examples: population sequencing pipelines, single-cell RNA-seq analysis methods
explicitly_excludes: Pure methods papers in CS without biological application (Computer Science)

subspecialties.name: Proteomics & Metabolomics
subspecialties.tagline: Global protein and metabolite measurement and interpretation
covers: mass spectrometry workflows, quantitative proteomics/metabolomics analysis
examples: phosphoproteomics signaling studies, metabolite profiling in cells

uncovered_concerns: Where to place evolutionary molecular work: 'Evolutionary Biology' often spans level-1 Ecology & Evolution; to keep this biology picker focused on mechanistic lab work I excluded broad evolutionary/ecology subspecialties.
Neuroscience and clinical translational immunology/genetics were intentionally routed out to Brain & Cognitive Sciences and Medicine respectively to keep Biology MECE.
Computational-methods-only papers (pure CS/ML algorithm work) are excluded from Genomics & Computational Biology unless there is a clear biological application or dataset.

Some overlap is inevitable (e.g., microbial pathogenesis can be read by Microbiology or Immunology); I prioritized the primary audience of practitioners (mechanistic microbiologists → Microbiology; host-immune mechanism focus → Immunology).
